svg.title: Full mDoc ISO 18013-5 Structure — AV compliant (updated: deviceAuth, deviceKeyInfo, 2-level chain verification now implemented)
svg.text: top-level container sent to verifier ✗ not implemented
△ no wrapper struct — contents below implemented
ES256 (ECDSA P-256) | Issuer DS key pair
holder's OWN key signs — never the issuer's
ES256 (ECDSA P-256) | key: Holder device key pair
trust = X.509 chain, not the sig
holder public key — embedded at issuance
all three change every session — that's the point
Annex A §4.1.2 — "SHALL NOT include any other attribute"
family_name, birth_date etc. NOT permitted in eu.europa.ec.av.1
Two signatures — two ECDSA operations, two key pairs
proves: "data is authentic, from a trusted issuer"
proves: "this device, right now, holds the bound private key"
Both must pass — issuerAuth alone can be replayed by anyone who copied the credential.
deviceAuth ties the presentation to a live session on the original device.
✓ both verified in code — see TestDeviceAuthWrongSignerIsRejected / TestDeviceAuthWrongSessionIsRejected
deviceKeyInfo = binding (issuer vouches, once) | deviceAuth = authentication (holder proves it, live)
binding says: "this key belongs to this credential" | auth proves: "I am that key, right now"
Who generates the device key pair?
✓ the HOLDER generates it locally — ideally inside Secure Enclave / TEE
✓ only the PUBLIC key is sent to the issuer, embedded in MSO.deviceKeyInfo
✗ issuer NEVER sees the private key — would defeat device binding entirely
privateKey stays on device forever, used fresh each presentation
publicKey locked into MSO once, signed by issuerAuth, trusted forever
recall: this is exactly the TrustZone/StrongBox boundary discussed earlier
sent from verifier → holder | specifies which attributes to reveal
} ← false = intent NOT to retain data (privacy flag)
QR code contains verifier ephemeral public key
Wallet scans QR → ECDH(ephemeral keys) → shared secret
[ mDoc session: AES-256-GCM ] ← primary, wraps DeviceRequest/Response
[ BLE link: AES-128-CCM ] ← radio layer underneath
Sig_structure — the bytes ECDSA actually signs
NOT the bare MSO payload — built fresh at sign/verify time
the actual thing being signed — Tag24(MSO) for
d = private key — never the payload itself
two numbers, 64 bytes total for P-256
payload still travels separately, in the clear —
the signature is a fingerprint of it, not a container
hashing bare MSO bytes alone → fails every time
Multipaz: keys carry ESP256 internally, but writes ES256 (−7)
Annex / IACA terminology — what we called "trusted CA list" earlier
COSE_Sign1 signature valid over Sig_structure, using DS public key
DS cert in x5chain chains to a trusted IACA root
✓ implemented — dsCert.Verify() with real IACA→DS 2-level chain
Each disclosed item's Tag-24 digest matches ValueDigests entry
all three steps now enforced — TestUntrustedRootIsRejected confirms rejection of untrusted chains

Full ISO 18013-5 mDoc Structure — solid border = implemented in Go | dashed orange = spec only | amber = Sig_structure detail | green = trust chain + deviceAuth (now implemented) | still missing: DeviceResponse/DeviceRequest wrapper, BLE session encryption
